terms.definition: A foundational attempt by the Progressive movement to rationalize social morality and institutionalize virtue through federal power and oversight.
A religious movement that applied Christian ethics to social problems, emphasizing systemic societal reform over individual sin.
A 1910 federal law criminalizing the interstate transportation of women for 'immoral purposes,' used to regulate personal sexual morality.
A self-regulatory body established by the film industry in 1909 to preempt state control and censor films deemed morally objectionable.
The process by which industries or organizations adopt self-regulation to avoid direct governmental intervention.
